questions.question: Does src/store/task-reducer.property.test.ts exist and assert all required invariants (S1, S2, S3, S4, S8, S9, A1, A2, A3, A4, A5, A6, A13, R1)?
questions.expected: At least one line per invariant ID in brackets at the start of an it() title

questions.question: Does each `it` title start with `[ID]` and each body begin with `// ID:` comment?
verification_commands: grep -n '// S[0-9]\|// A[0-9]\|// R1\|// AC-X' src/store/task-reducer.property.test.ts
questions.expected: All it() titles open with [ID]; first line of each body is a // comment matching the ID

questions.question: Does `npx vitest run` pass with the new file included, and is the test count 48+N (where N=16)?
With FAST_CHECK_NUM_RUNS=10000, do all properties still pass?
questions.verification_command: FAST_CHECK_NUM_RUNS=10000 npx vitest run src/store/task-reducer.property.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -E 'Tests|failed'

questions.question: Are the arbitraries state-aware with no >50% no-op rate? The `interpretStep` function maps kindIndex 0-1 (2/13 ≈ 15%) to task.created and 2-12 (11/13 ≈ 85%) to mutation events. When state is non-empty, less than 16% of steps result in task.created — the no-op rate for mutation events is near zero because they always target an existing task with a valid (non-stale by default) timestamp.
questions.expected: kindIndex distribution shows 2/13 for creates, 11/13 for mutations; no 80%+ no-op path

questions.question: No production code changed? Only the new test file plus the epic artifacts are new/modified.
Does the R1 resurrection test use fc.statistics to report the resurrection rate before the fc.assert?
questions.expected: fc.statistics call appears before the fc.assert in the [R1] test body, returning 'resurrected' or 'absent'

questions.question: Does the S8 test correctly verify reference-identity isolation (not just structural equality) to catch any aliasing bug in the in-memory shim?
questions.expected: expect(loaded1).not.toContain(e) — reference check via Array#includes (no serialization)

questions.question: Does the S4 test correctly exclude task.snapshot events (which reset all state and would break the max-tracker invariant)?
questions.expected: Comment noting that task.snapshot is excluded from arbEventSequence; no snapshot events generated by interpretStep
